title: Street Veg Cutlet

subs.name: finely diced boiled beetroot adds colour and a mild sweetness to the mix
rice flour as a binder keeps the mixture gluten-free; add an extra tablespoon if the mixture feels loose
finely crushed cornflakes or rice flakes give an even crispier crust and make the cutlet gluten-free

steps.text: Combine the mashed potato, grated boiled carrot, and boiled peas in a large bowl. Add chopped onion, green chilli, grated ginger, coriander leaves, red chilli powder, garam masala, amchur, and salt. Mix everything together thoroughly with your hands. Add cornflour as a binder and knead until the mixture holds together firmly when you press a handful — it should not be sticky. Taste and adjust salt and spice.

Divide the mixture into 8 equal portions. Shape each portion into a flat oval or round patty about 1.5 cm thick and 7 cm across, pressing firmly so the edges are smooth and crack-free. If the mixture feels too soft to hold shape, refrigerate the shaped patties for 15 minutes before coating.

Spread the fine rava on a flat plate. Press each shaped patty firmly into the rava on both sides and around the edges, coating evenly. Shake off any loose excess. The rava coating is what creates the crisp golden crust during frying.

Heat a tawa or heavy frying pan over medium flame. Drizzle 2 tablespoons of oil and spread it across the surface. When the oil is hot and shimmering, arrange the cutlets in a single layer without touching. Fry for 4–5 minutes, pressing down gently with a spatula, until the underside is a deep amber colour and the rava coating is crunchy. Flip carefully and add a drizzle more oil around each cutlet. Fry the second side for 3–4 minutes until equally golden. Avoid high heat — the cutlet needs time to heat through without the crust burning.

Transfer to a serving plate and serve hot with tomato ketchup, mint-coriander chutney, or any favourite dipping sauce. Veg cutlets are at their best straight from the pan when the rava shell is crackling. Leftovers can be re-crisped on a dry tawa for 2–3 minutes per side.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: rava → finely crushed cornflakes or rice flakes give an even crispier crust and make the cutlet gluten-free; carrot → finely diced boiled beetroot adds colour and a mild sweetness to the mix; cornflour → rice flour as a binder keeps the mixture gluten-free; add an extra tablespoon if the mixture feels loose
